frameworks.name: successor representation
frameworks.one_line: encodes states by their predictive relationships with future states

frameworks.name: reinforcement learning
frameworks.one_line: algorithms by which an agent learns long-term future reward

frameworks.name: model-free vs model-based tradeoff
frameworks.one_line: successor representation negotiates efficiency-flexibility tradeoff between the two

core_question: What state representation should the brain use to support reinforcement learning given computational constraints?
key_assumptions: RL algorithms operate over learned state and action representations
brain architecture imposes constraints on representational choice
efficiency and flexibility are competing demands on RL
key_findings: behavioral evidence supports use of successor-representation-like predictive codes
neural evidence supports successor-representation-like predictive codes
computational extensions broaden the original successor representation idea
interpretations: the successor representation organizes RL by balancing efficiency against flexibility
predictive state encoding is a useful framework for brain RL
